title: feat: upgrade glm slug to GLM-5.2
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Repoints the existing `glm` slug from GLM-5.1 to **GLM-5.2** (live day-zero on Fireworks).

- **modelId** → `accounts/fireworks/models/glm-5p2`
- **Pricing** (Fireworks list, `priceMultiplier: 1` at-cost, matching 5.1): $1.40 in / $0.26 cached / $4.40 out per 1M (was $1.0 / $0.2 / $3.2)
- **Title** → Z.ai GLM-5.2; **contextLength** → 1048576 (full 1M Fireworks lists; 5.1 was conservatively 198k)
- Kept `glm-5.1` / `glm-5p1` / `glm-5` as aliases — existing callers still resolve
- `addedDate` untouched (in-place upgrade of an existing slug, not a new model)

In-place upgrade follows how this slug already rolled 4.7→5→5.1. **Note: everyone calling `glm` now bills ~40% more per token.**

### Verification
- `aliases.test.ts` (211), `pricing-data.test.ts` (6), `model-permissions.test.ts` (3) pass
- `effective-prices.snapshot.test.ts` updated — diff is GLM-only (6 lines: cost+price blocks)
- Empirical: probed `accounts/fireworks/models/glm-5p2` directly on Fireworks — 200, coherent output, usage block maps cleanly to our 3 cost keys (no unmapped fields → field-parity holds)
- biome clean; no TS errors in touched files

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: A million tokens of GLM
gist.blurb: We've upgraded the default GLM model to Z.ai's brand new GLM-5.2. Enjoy a massive 1M token context window for your biggest, most complex prompts.
gist.summary: Upgraded the `glm` model slug to point to Z.ai's GLM-5.2 via Fireworks, expanding the context window from 198k to a full 1M tokens.
gist.impact: Users calling the `glm` endpoint will automatically use the new 5.2 model and its 1M context window, with pricing updated to Fireworks' at-cost rates ($1.40 in / $4.40 out per 1M tokens).
gist.visual_concept: The pollinations bee and the monitor robot reading an endlessly long scroll of text or code, representing the massive 1M token context window. A '5.2' label on a retro terminal.
